revision.text: {| class="wikitable" style="width: auto; text-align: left;" |+ {{Navbar-header|Wiktionary link templates|Wiktionary templates common doc|plain=1}} ! Template !! Alias !! Explanation |- | {{tl|Wiktionary}} || {{tl|wikt}} || Makes a box. |- | {{tl|Wiktionary pipe}} || {{tl|wtp}} || Ditto. Allows a piped link. |- | {{tl|See Wiktionary}} || || A disambiguation hatnote type. Useful if the article title is a generic name, but the content differs from it. For example, [[Tryout]] is an article about a journal, and this template is used to link to "tryout" page in Wiktionary. |- | {{tl|Wiktionary-inline}} || {{tl|wti}} || Can be used in the "External links" section, by making a one-line navigator. Not inline in the usual sense. |- | {{tl|Wiktionary redirect}} || {{tl|wtr}} || Makes a [[Wikipedia:soft redirect|soft redirect]]. |- | {{nowrap|{{tl|Wiktionary category 2}}}} || {{tl|wtc}} || Similar to {{tl|wikt}}, but links to a category in Wiktionary. |- | {{tl|Wiktionary category}} || || Ditto, more verbose type. |- | {{tl|Linktext}} || || Turns each of consecutive words into an Wiktionary link, or any other interwiki / interlang link. Example: {{tnull|linktext|táłtłʼááh| adijiłii}}  →  {{linktext|táłtłʼááh| adijiłii}} |- | {{tl|wt}} || || Links to a language-specific section of a Wiktionary entry. Deprecated ([[Wikipedia:Templates for discussion/Log/2020 October 25#Template:Wt|discussion log]]). |- | {{tl|wikt-lang}} || || Applies a language tag to a word, and links to that language's section of the Wiktionary entry on the word, much like the [[wikt:Template:m|{{((}}m{{))}}]] and [[wikt:Template:l|{{((}}l{{))}}]] templates on Wiktionary. For instance, {{tnull|1=wikt-lang|2=en|3=be|4=was}} yields {{wikt-lang|en|be|was}}, and is equivalent to {{lang|en|[[wikt:be#English|was]]}}. |} Moving a non-encyclopedic entry to Wiktionary: * {{tl|Transwikied to Wiktionary}}, {{tl|Transwiki to Wiktionary Finished}}: Pasted at the top of the talk page by admins. Markup: * [[wikt:ᐁᕕᖃ]] → [[wikt:ᐁᕕᖃ]] * [[wikt:粘菌|'myxomycete' in Japanese]] → [[wikt:粘菌|'myxomycete' in Japanese]] (You can use a pipe as usual.) * [[wikt:curcubeu|]] → [[wikt:curcubeu|curcubeu]] (You can hide "wikt:" by a [[Help:pipe trick|pipe trick]].) Wiktionary-related userboxes: * {{tl|User Wiktionary}}: "This user has a page on Wiktionary" box. * {{tl|User admin Wiktionary}}: "This user is an administrator on Wiktionary" box. See also: * [[Wikipedia:Wikipedia is not a dictionary]] [[Category:Wiktionary link templates| ]] [[Category:Documentation see also templates]]

{| class="wikitable" style="width: auto; text-align: left;" |+ {{Navbar-header|Wiktionary link templates|Wiktionary templates common doc|plain=1}} ! Template !! Alias !! Explanation |- | {{tl|Wiktionary}} || {{tl|wikt}} || Makes a box. |- | {{tl|Wiktionary pipe}} || {{tl|wtp}} || Ditto. Allows a piped link. |- | {{tl|See Wiktionary}} || || A disambiguation hatnote type. Useful if the article title is a generic name, but the content differs from it. For example, [[Tryout]] is an article about a journal, and this template is used to link to "tryout" page in Wiktionary. |- | {{tl|Wiktionary-inline}} || {{tl|wti}} || Can be used in the "External links" section, by making a one-line navigator. Not inline in the usual sense. |- | {{tl|Wiktionary redirect}} || {{tl|wtr}} || Makes a [[Wikipedia:soft redirect|soft redirect]]. |- | {{nowrap|{{tl|Wiktionary category 2}}}} || {{tl|wtc}} || Similar to {{tl|wikt}}, but links to a category in Wiktionary. |- | {{tl|Wiktionary category}} || || Ditto, more verbose type. |- | {{tl|Linktext}} || || Turns each of consecutive words into an Wiktionary link, or any other interwiki / interlang link. Example: {{tnull|linktext|táłtłʼááh| adijiłii}}  →  {{linktext|táłtłʼááh| adijiłii}} |- | {{tl|wt}} || || Links to a language-specific section of a Wiktionary entry. Deprecated ([[Wikipedia:Templates for discussion/Log/2020 October 25#Template:Wt|discussion log]]). |- | {{tl|wikt-lang}} || || Applies a language tag to a word, and links to that language's section of the Wiktionary entry on the word, much like the [[wikt:Template:m|{{((}}m{{))}}]] and [[wikt:Template:l|{{((}}l{{))}}]] templates on Wiktionary. For instance, {{tnull|1=wikt-lang|2=en|3=be|4=was}} yields {{wikt-lang|en|be|was}}, and is equivalent to {{lang|en|[[wikt:be#English|was]]}}. |} Moving a non-encyclopedic entry to Wiktionary: * {{tl|Transwikied to Wiktionary}}, {{tl|Transwiki to Wiktionary Finished}}: Pasted at the top of the talk page by admins. Markup: * [[wikt:ᐁᕕᖃ]] → [[wikt:ᐁᕕᖃ]] * [[wikt:粘菌|'myxomycete' in Japanese]] → [[wikt:粘菌|'myxomycete' in Japanese]] (You can use a pipe as usual.) * [[wikt:curcubeu|]] → [[wikt:curcubeu|curcubeu]] (You can hide "wikt:" by a [[Help:pipe trick|pipe trick]].) Wiktionary-related userboxes: * {{tl|User Wiktionary}}: "This user has a page on Wiktionary" box. * {{tl|User admin Wiktionary}}: "This user is an administrator on Wiktionary" box. See also: * [[Wikipedia:Wikipedia is not a dictionary]] [[Category:Wiktionary link templates| ]] [[Category:Documentation see also templates]]

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:pages from wikipedia]][[en:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:pages from wikipedia]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text: {| class="wikitable" style="width: auto; text-align: left;" |+ {{Navbar-header|Wiktionary link templates|Wiktionary templates common doc|plain=1}} ! Template !! Alias !! Explanation |- | {{tl|Wiktionary}} || {{tl|wikt}} || Makes a box. |- | {{tl|Wiktionary pipe}} || {{tl|wtp}} || Ditto. Allows a piped link. |- | {{tl|See Wiktionary}} || || A disambiguation hatnote type. Useful if the article title is a generic name, but the content differs from it. For example, [[Tryout]] is an article about a journal, and this template is used to link to "tryout" page in Wiktionary. |- | {{tl|Wiktionary-inline}} || {{tl|wti}} || Can be used in the "External links" section, by making a one-line navigator. Not inline in the usual sense. |- | {{tl|Wiktionary redirect}} || {{tl|wtr}} || Makes a [[Wikipedia:soft redirect|soft redirect]]. |- | {{nowrap|{{tl|Wiktionary category 2}}}} || {{tl|wtc}} || Similar to {{tl|wikt}}, but links to a category in Wiktionary. |- | {{tl|Wiktionary category}} || || Ditto, more verbose type. |- | {{tl|Linktext}} || || Turns each of consecutive words into an Wiktionary link, or any other interwiki / interlang link. Example: {{tnull|linktext|táłtłʼááh| adijiłii}}  →  {{linktext|táłtłʼááh| adijiłii}} |- | {{tl|wt}} || || Links to a language-specific section of a Wiktionary entry. Deprecated ([[Wikipedia:Templates for discussion/Log/2020 October 25#Template:Wt|discussion log]]). |- | {{tl|wikt-lang}} || || Applies a language tag to a word, and links to that language's section of the Wiktionary entry on the word, much like the [[wikt:Template:m|{{((}}m{{))}}]] and [[wikt:Template:l|{{((}}l{{))}}]] templates on Wiktionary. For instance, {{tnull|1=wikt-lang|2=en|3=be|4=was}} yields {{wikt-lang|en|be|was}}, and is equivalent to {{lang|en|[[wikt:be#English|was]]}}. |} Moving a non-encyclopedic entry to Wiktionary: * {{tl|Transwikied to Wiktionary}}, {{tl|Transwiki to Wiktionary Finished}}: Pasted at the top of the talk page by admins. Markup: * [[wikt:ᐁᕕᖃ]] → [[wikt:ᐁᕕᖃ]] * [[wikt:粘菌|'myxomycete' in Japanese]] → [[wikt:粘菌|'myxomycete' in Japanese]] (You can use a pipe as usual.) * [[wikt:curcubeu|]] → [[wikt:curcubeu|curcubeu]] (You can hide "wikt:" by a [[Help:pipe trick|pipe trick]].) Wiktionary-related userboxes: * {{tl|User Wiktionary}}: "This user has a page on Wiktionary" box. * {{tl|User admin Wiktionary}}: "This user is an administrator on Wiktionary" box. See also: * [[Wikipedia:Wikipedia is not a dictionary]] [[Category:Wiktionary link templates| ]] [[Category:Documentation see also templates]] [[Category:pages from wikipedia]][[en:Wiktionary templates common doc]][[Category:pages from wikipedia]]

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:pages from wikipedia]][[en:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:pages from wikipedia]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text: {| class="wikitable" style="width: auto; text-align: left;" |+ {{Navbar-header|Wiktionary link templates|Wiktionary templates common doc|plain=1}} ! Template !! Alias !! Explanation |- | {{tl|Wiktionary}} || {{tl|wikt}} || Makes a box. |- | {{tl|Wiktionary pipe}} || {{tl|wtp}} || Ditto. Allows a piped link. |- | {{tl|See Wiktionary}} || || A disambiguation hatnote type. Useful if the article title is a generic name, but the content differs from it. For example, [[Tryout]] is an article about a journal, and this template is used to link to "tryout" page in Wiktionary. |- | {{tl|Wiktionary-inline}} || {{tl|wti}} || Can be used in the "External links" section, by making a one-line navigator. Not inline in the usual sense. |- | {{tl|Wiktionary redirect}} || {{tl|wtr}} || Makes a [[Wikipedia:soft redirect|soft redirect]]. |- | {{nowrap|{{tl|Wiktionary category 2}}}} || {{tl|wtc}} || Similar to {{tl|wikt}}, but links to a category in Wiktionary. |- | {{tl|Wiktionary category}} || || Ditto, more verbose type. |- | {{tl|Linktext}} || || Turns each of consecutive words into an Wiktionary link, or any other interwiki / interlang link. Example: {{tnull|linktext|táłtłʼááh| adijiłii}}  →  {{linktext|táłtłʼááh| adijiłii}} |- | {{tl|wt}} || || Links to a language-specific section of a Wiktionary entry. Deprecated ([[Wikipedia:Templates for discussion/Log/2020 October 25#Template:Wt|discussion log]]). |- | {{tl|wikt-lang}} || || Applies a language tag to a word, and links to that language's section of the Wiktionary entry on the word, much like the [[wikt:Template:m|{{((}}m{{))}}]] and [[wikt:Template:l|{{((}}l{{))}}]] templates on Wiktionary. For instance, {{tnull|1=wikt-lang|2=en|3=be|4=was}} yields {{wikt-lang|en|be|was}}, and is equivalent to {{lang|en|[[wikt:be#English|was]]}}. |} Moving a non-encyclopedic entry to Wiktionary: * {{tl|Transwikied to Wiktionary}}, {{tl|Transwiki to Wiktionary Finished}}: Pasted at the top of the talk page by admins. Markup: * [[wikt:ᐁᕕᖃ]] → [[wikt:ᐁᕕᖃ]] * [[wikt:粘菌|'myxomycete' in Japanese]] → [[wikt:粘菌|'myxomycete' in Japanese]] (You can use a pipe as usual.) * [[wikt:curcubeu|]] → [[wikt:curcubeu|curcubeu]] (You can hide "wikt:" by a [[Help:pipe trick|pipe trick]].) Wiktionary-related userboxes: * {{tl|User Wiktionary}}: "This user has a page on Wiktionary" box. * {{tl|User admin Wiktionary}}: "This user is an administrator on Wiktionary" box. See also: * [[Wikipedia:Wikipedia is not a dictionary]] [[Category:Wiktionary link templates| ]] [[Category:Documentation see also templates]] [[Category:pages from wikipedia]][[en:Wiktionary templates common doc]][[Category:pages from wikipedia]] [[Category:pages from wikipedia]][[en:Wiktionary templates common doc]][[Category:pages from wikipedia]]

revision.comment: Moving from [[Category:Pages from wikipedia]] to [[Category:Improperly tagged templates and modules]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text: {| class="wikitable" style="width: auto; text-align: left;" |+ {{Navbar-header|Wiktionary link templates|Wiktionary templates common doc|plain=1}} ! Template !! Alias !! Explanation |- | {{tl|Wiktionary}} || {{tl|wikt}} || Makes a box. |- | {{tl|Wiktionary pipe}} || {{tl|wtp}} || Ditto. Allows a piped link. |- | {{tl|See Wiktionary}} || || A disambiguation hatnote type. Useful if the article title is a generic name, but the content differs from it. For example, [[Tryout]] is an article about a journal, and this template is used to link to "tryout" page in Wiktionary. |- | {{tl|Wiktionary-inline}} || {{tl|wti}} || Can be used in the "External links" section, by making a one-line navigator. Not inline in the usual sense. |- | {{tl|Wiktionary redirect}} || {{tl|wtr}} || Makes a [[Wikipedia:soft redirect|soft redirect]]. |- | {{nowrap|{{tl|Wiktionary category 2}}}} || {{tl|wtc}} || Similar to {{tl|wikt}}, but links to a category in Wiktionary. |- | {{tl|Wiktionary category}} || || Ditto, more verbose type. |- | {{tl|Linktext}} || || Turns each of consecutive words into an Wiktionary link, or any other interwiki / interlang link. Example: {{tnull|linktext|táłtłʼááh| adijiłii}}  →  {{linktext|táłtłʼááh| adijiłii}} |- | {{tl|wt}} || || Links to a language-specific section of a Wiktionary entry. Deprecated ([[Wikipedia:Templates for discussion/Log/2020 October 25#Template:Wt|discussion log]]). |- | {{tl|wikt-lang}} || || Applies a language tag to a word, and links to that language's section of the Wiktionary entry on the word, much like the [[wikt:Template:m|{{((}}m{{))}}]] and [[wikt:Template:l|{{((}}l{{))}}]] templates on Wiktionary. For instance, {{tnull|1=wikt-lang|2=en|3=be|4=was}} yields {{wikt-lang|en|be|was}}, and is equivalent to {{lang|en|[[wikt:be#English|was]]}}. |} Moving a non-encyclopedic entry to Wiktionary: * {{tl|Transwikied to Wiktionary}}, {{tl|Transwiki to Wiktionary Finished}}: Pasted at the top of the talk page by admins. Markup: * [[wikt:ᐁᕕᖃ]] → [[wikt:ᐁᕕᖃ]] * [[wikt:粘菌|'myxomycete' in Japanese]] → [[wikt:粘菌|'myxomycete' in Japanese]] (You can use a pipe as usual.) * [[wikt:curcubeu|]] → [[wikt:curcubeu|curcubeu]] (You can hide "wikt:" by a [[Help:pipe trick|pipe trick]].) Wiktionary-related userboxes: * {{tl|User Wiktionary}}: "This user has a page on Wiktionary" box. * {{tl|User admin Wiktionary}}: "This user is an administrator on Wiktionary" box. See also: * [[Wikipedia:Wikipedia is not a dictionary]] [[Category:Wiktionary link templates| ]] [[Category:Documentation see also templates]] [[Category:Improperly tagged templates and modules]] [[en:Wiktionary templates common doc]][[Category:Improperly tagged templates and modules]] [[Category:Improperly tagged templates and modules]] [[en:Wiktionary templates common doc]][[Category:Improperly tagged templates and modules]]

revision.comment: Moving from [[Category:Improperly tagged templates and modules]] to [[Category:Improperly tagged templates and modules2]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text: {| class="wikitable" style="width: auto; text-align: left;" |+ {{Navbar-header|Wiktionary link templates|Wiktionary templates common doc|plain=1}} ! Template !! Alias !! Explanation |- | {{tl|Wiktionary}} || {{tl|wikt}} || Makes a box. |- | {{tl|Wiktionary pipe}} || {{tl|wtp}} || Ditto. Allows a piped link. |- | {{tl|See Wiktionary}} || || A disambiguation hatnote type. Useful if the article title is a generic name, but the content differs from it. For example, [[Tryout]] is an article about a journal, and this template is used to link to "tryout" page in Wiktionary. |- | {{tl|Wiktionary-inline}} || {{tl|wti}} || Can be used in the "External links" section, by making a one-line navigator. Not inline in the usual sense. |- | {{tl|Wiktionary redirect}} || {{tl|wtr}} || Makes a [[Wikipedia:soft redirect|soft redirect]]. |- | {{nowrap|{{tl|Wiktionary category 2}}}} || {{tl|wtc}} || Similar to {{tl|wikt}}, but links to a category in Wiktionary. |- | {{tl|Wiktionary category}} || || Ditto, more verbose type. |- | {{tl|Linktext}} || || Turns each of consecutive words into an Wiktionary link, or any other interwiki / interlang link. Example: {{tnull|linktext|táłtłʼááh| adijiłii}}  →  {{linktext|táłtłʼááh| adijiłii}} |- | {{tl|wt}} || || Links to a language-specific section of a Wiktionary entry. Deprecated ([[Wikipedia:Templates for discussion/Log/2020 October 25#Template:Wt|discussion log]]). |- | {{tl|wikt-lang}} || || Applies a language tag to a word, and links to that language's section of the Wiktionary entry on the word, much like the [[wikt:Template:m|{{((}}m{{))}}]] and [[wikt:Template:l|{{((}}l{{))}}]] templates on Wiktionary. For instance, {{tnull|1=wikt-lang|2=en|3=be|4=was}} yields {{wikt-lang|en|be|was}}, and is equivalent to {{lang|en|[[wikt:be#English|was]]}}. |} Moving a non-encyclopedic entry to Wiktionary: * {{tl|Transwikied to Wiktionary}}, {{tl|Transwiki to Wiktionary Finished}}: Pasted at the top of the talk page by admins. Markup: * [[wikt:ᐁᕕᖃ]] → [[wikt:ᐁᕕᖃ]] * [[wikt:粘菌|'myxomycete' in Japanese]] → [[wikt:粘菌|'myxomycete' in Japanese]] (You can use a pipe as usual.) * [[wikt:curcubeu|]] → [[wikt:curcubeu|curcubeu]] (You can hide "wikt:" by a [[Help:pipe trick|pipe trick]].) Wiktionary-related userboxes: * {{tl|User Wiktionary}}: "This user has a page on Wiktionary" box. * {{tl|User admin Wiktionary}}: "This user is an administrator on Wiktionary" box. See also: * [[Wikipedia:Wikipedia is not a dictionary]] [[Category:Wiktionary link templates| ]] [[Category:Documentation see also templates]] [[Category:Improperly tagged templates and modules2]] [[en:Wiktionary templates common doc]][[Category:Improperly tagged templates and modules2]] [[Category:Improperly tagged templates and modules2]] [[en:Wiktionary templates common doc]][[Category:Improperly tagged templates and modules2]]

revision.comment: Moving from [[Category:Improperly tagged templates and modules2]] to [[Category:Improperly tagged templates and modules]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text: {| class="wikitable" style="width: auto; text-align: left;" |+ {{Navbar-header|Wiktionary link templates|Wiktionary templates common doc|plain=1}} ! Template !! Alias !! Explanation |- | {{tl|Wiktionary}} || {{tl|wikt}} || Makes a box. |- | {{tl|Wiktionary pipe}} || {{tl|wtp}} || Ditto. Allows a piped link. |- | {{tl|See Wiktionary}} || || A disambiguation hatnote type. Useful if the article title is a generic name, but the content differs from it. For example, [[Tryout]] is an article about a journal, and this template is used to link to "tryout" page in Wiktionary. |- | {{tl|Wiktionary-inline}} || {{tl|wti}} || Can be used in the "External links" section, by making a one-line navigator. Not inline in the usual sense. |- | {{tl|Wiktionary redirect}} || {{tl|wtr}} || Makes a [[Wikipedia:soft redirect|soft redirect]]. |- | {{nowrap|{{tl|Wiktionary category 2}}}} || {{tl|wtc}} || Similar to {{tl|wikt}}, but links to a category in Wiktionary. |- | {{tl|Wiktionary category}} || || Ditto, more verbose type. |- | {{tl|Linktext}} || || Turns each of consecutive words into an Wiktionary link, or any other interwiki / interlang link. Example: {{tnull|linktext|táłtłʼááh| adijiłii}}  →  {{linktext|táłtłʼááh| adijiłii}} |- | {{tl|wt}} || || Links to a language-specific section of a Wiktionary entry. Deprecated ([[Wikipedia:Templates for discussion/Log/2020 October 25#Template:Wt|discussion log]]). |- | {{tl|wikt-lang}} || || Applies a language tag to a word, and links to that language's section of the Wiktionary entry on the word, much like the [[wikt:Template:m|{{((}}m{{))}}]] and [[wikt:Template:l|{{((}}l{{))}}]] templates on Wiktionary. For instance, {{tnull|1=wikt-lang|2=en|3=be|4=was}} yields {{wikt-lang|en|be|was}}, and is equivalent to {{lang|en|[[wikt:be#English|was]]}}. |} Moving a non-encyclopedic entry to Wiktionary: * {{tl|Transwikied to Wiktionary}}, {{tl|Transwiki to Wiktionary Finished}}: Pasted at the top of the talk page by admins. Markup: * [[wikt:ᐁᕕᖃ]] → [[wikt:ᐁᕕᖃ]] * [[wikt:粘菌|'myxomycete' in Japanese]] → [[wikt:粘菌|'myxomycete' in Japanese]] (You can use a pipe as usual.) * [[wikt:curcubeu|]] → [[wikt:curcubeu|curcubeu]] (You can hide "wikt:" by a [[Help:pipe trick|pipe trick]].) Wiktionary-related userboxes: * {{tl|User Wiktionary}}: "This user has a page on Wiktionary" box. * {{tl|User admin Wiktionary}}: "This user is an administrator on Wiktionary" box. See also: * [[Wikipedia:Wikipedia is not a dictionary]] [[Category:Wiktionary link templates| ]] [[Category:Documentation see also templates]] [[Category:Improperly tagged templates and modules]] [[en:Wiktionary templates common doc]][[Category:Improperly tagged templates and modules]] [[Category:Improperly tagged templates and modules]] [[en:Wiktionary templates common doc]][[Category:Improperly tagged templates and modules]]

revision.comment: Trimmed off cruft from entries in [[:Category:Improperly tagged templates and modules]]
revision.text: {| class="wikitable" style="width: auto; text-align: left;" |+ {{Navbar-header|Wiktionary link templates|Wiktionary templates common doc|plain=1}} ! Template !! Alias !! Explanation |- | {{tl|Wiktionary}} || {{tl|wikt}} || Makes a box. |- | {{tl|Wiktionary pipe}} || {{tl|wtp}} || Ditto. Allows a piped link. |- | {{tl|See Wiktionary}} || || A disambiguation hatnote type. Useful if the article title is a generic name, but the content differs from it. For example, [[Tryout]] is an article about a journal, and this template is used to link to "tryout" page in Wiktionary. |- | {{tl|Wiktionary-inline}} || {{tl|wti}} || Can be used in the "External links" section, by making a one-line navigator. Not inline in the usual sense. |- | {{tl|Wiktionary redirect}} || {{tl|wtr}} || Makes a [[Wikipedia:soft redirect|soft redirect]]. |- | {{nowrap|{{tl|Wiktionary category 2}}}} || {{tl|wtc}} || Similar to {{tl|wikt}}, but links to a category in Wiktionary. |- | {{tl|Wiktionary category}} || || Ditto, more verbose type. |- | {{tl|Linktext}} || || Turns each of consecutive words into an Wiktionary link, or any other interwiki / interlang link. Example: {{tnull|linktext|táłtłʼááh| adijiłii}}  →  {{linktext|táłtłʼááh| adijiłii}} |- | {{tl|wt}} || || Links to a language-specific section of a Wiktionary entry. Deprecated ([[Wikipedia:Templates for discussion/Log/2020 October 25#Template:Wt|discussion log]]). |- | {{tl|wikt-lang}} || || Applies a language tag to a word, and links to that language's section of the Wiktionary entry on the word, much like the [[wikt:Template:m|{{((}}m{{))}}]] and [[wikt:Template:l|{{((}}l{{))}}]] templates on Wiktionary. For instance, {{tnull|1=wikt-lang|2=en|3=be|4=was}} yields {{wikt-lang|en|be|was}}, and is equivalent to {{lang|en|[[wikt:be#English|was]]}}. |} Moving a non-encyclopedic entry to Wiktionary: * {{tl|Transwikied to Wiktionary}}, {{tl|Transwiki to Wiktionary Finished}}: Pasted at the top of the talk page by admins. Markup: * [[wikt:ᐁᕕᖃ]] → [[wikt:ᐁᕕᖃ]] * [[wikt:粘菌|'myxomycete' in Japanese]] → [[wikt:粘菌|'myxomycete' in Japanese]] (You can use a pipe as usual.) * [[wikt:curcubeu|]] → [[wikt:curcubeu|curcubeu]] (You can hide "wikt:" by a [[Help:pipe trick|pipe trick]].) Wiktionary-related userboxes: * {{tl|User Wiktionary}}: "This user has a page on Wiktionary" box. * {{tl|User admin Wiktionary}}: "This user is an administrator on Wiktionary" box. See also: * [[Wikipedia:Wikipedia is not a dictionary]] [[Category:Wiktionary link templates| ]] [[Category:Documentation see also templates]]

{| class="wikitable" style="width: auto; text-align: left;" |+ {{Navbar-header|Wiktionary link templates|Wiktionary templates common doc|plain=1}} ! Template !! Alias !! Explanation |- | {{tl|Wiktionary}} || {{tl|wikt}} || Makes a box. |- | {{tl|Wiktionary pipe}} || {{tl|wtp}} || Ditto. Allows a piped link. |- | {{tl|See Wiktionary}} || || A disambiguation hatnote type. Useful if the article title is a generic name, but the content differs from it. For example, [[Tryout]] is an article about a journal, and this template is used to link to "tryout" page in Wiktionary. |- | {{tl|Wiktionary-inline}} || {{tl|wti}} || Can be used in the "External links" section, by making a one-line navigator. Not inline in the usual sense. |- | {{tl|Wiktionary redirect}} || {{tl|wtr}} || Makes a [[Wikipedia:soft redirect|soft redirect]]. |- | {{nowrap|{{tl|Wiktionary category 2}}}} || {{tl|wtc}} || Similar to {{tl|wikt}}, but links to a category in Wiktionary. |- | {{tl|Wiktionary category}} || || Ditto, more verbose type. |- | {{tl|Linktext}} || || Turns each of consecutive words into an Wiktionary link, or any other interwiki / interlang link. Example: {{tnull|linktext|táłtłʼááh| adijiłii}}  →  {{linktext|táłtłʼááh| adijiłii}} |- | {{tl|wt}} || || Links to a language-specific section of a Wiktionary entry. Deprecated ([[Wikipedia:Templates for discussion/Log/2020 October 25#Template:Wt|discussion log]]). |- | {{tl|wikt-lang}} || || Applies a language tag to a word, and links to that language's section of the Wiktionary entry on the word, much like the [[wikt:Template:m|{{((}}m{{))}}]] and [[wikt:Template:l|{{((}}l{{))}}]] templates on Wiktionary. For instance, {{tnull|1=wikt-lang|2=en|3=be|4=was}} yields {{wikt-lang|en|be|was}}, and is equivalent to {{lang|en|[[wikt:be#English|was]]}}. |} Moving a non-encyclopedic entry to Wiktionary: * {{tl|Transwikied to Wiktionary}}, {{tl|Transwiki to Wiktionary Finished}}: Pasted at the top of the talk page by admins. Markup: * [[wikt:ᐁᕕᖃ]] → [[wikt:ᐁᕕᖃ]] * [[wikt:粘菌|'myxomycete' in Japanese]] → [[wikt:粘菌|'myxomycete' in Japanese]] (You can use a pipe as usual.) * [[wikt:curcubeu|]] → [[wikt:curcubeu|curcubeu]] (You can hide "wikt:" by a [[Help:pipe trick|pipe trick]].) Wiktionary-related userboxes: * {{tl|User Wiktionary}}: "This user has a page on Wiktionary" box. * {{tl|User admin Wiktionary}}: "This user is an administrator on Wiktionary" box. See also: * [[Wikipedia:Wikipedia is not a dictionary]] [[Category:Wiktionary link templates| ]] [[Category:Documentation see also templates]]

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text: {| class="wikitable" style="width: auto; text-align: left;" |+ {{Navbar-header|Wiktionary link templates|Wiktionary templates common doc|plain=1}} ! Template !! Alias !! Explanation |- | {{tl|Wiktionary}} || {{tl|wikt}} || Makes a box. |- | {{tl|Wiktionary pipe}} || {{tl|wtp}} || Ditto. Allows a piped link. |- | {{tl|See Wiktionary}} || || A disambiguation hatnote type. Useful if the article title is a generic name, but the content differs from it. For example, [[:en:Tryout|Tryout]] is an article about a journal, and this template is used to link to "tryout" page in Wiktionary. |- | {{tl|Wiktionary-inline}} || {{tl|wti}} || Can be used in the "External links" section, by making a one-line navigator. Not inline in the usual sense. |- | {{tl|Wiktionary redirect}} || {{tl|wtr}} || Makes a [[Wikipedia:soft redirect|soft redirect]]. |- | {{nowrap|{{tl|Wiktionary category 2}}}} || {{tl|wtc}} || Similar to {{tl|wikt}}, but links to a category in Wiktionary. |- | {{tl|Wiktionary category}} || || Ditto, more verbose type. |- | {{tl|Linktext}} || || Turns each of consecutive words into an Wiktionary link, or any other interwiki / interlang link. Example: {{tnull|linktext|táłtłʼááh| adijiłii}}  →  {{linktext|táłtłʼááh| adijiłii}} |- | {{tl|wt}} || || Links to a language-specific section of a Wiktionary entry. Deprecated ([[Wikipedia:Templates for discussion/Log/2020 October 25#Template:Wt|discussion log]]). |- | {{tl|wikt-lang}} || || Applies a language tag to a word, and links to that language's section of the Wiktionary entry on the word, much like the [[wikt:Template:m|{{((}}m{{))}}]] and [[wikt:Template:l|{{((}}l{{))}}]] templates on Wiktionary. For instance, {{tnull|1=wikt-lang|2=en|3=be|4=was}} yields {{wikt-lang|en|be|was}}, and is equivalent to {{lang|en|[[wikt:be#English|was]]}}. |} Moving a non-encyclopedic entry to Wiktionary: * {{tl|Transwikied to Wiktionary}}, {{tl|Transwiki to Wiktionary Finished}}: Pasted at the top of the talk page by admins. Markup: * [[wikt:ᐁᕕᖃ]] → [[wikt:ᐁᕕᖃ]] * [[wikt:粘菌|'myxomycete' in Japanese]] → [[wikt:粘菌|'myxomycete' in Japanese]] (You can use a pipe as usual.) * [[wikt:curcubeu|]] → [[wikt:curcubeu|curcubeu]] (You can hide "wikt:" by a [[Help:pipe trick|pipe trick]].) Wiktionary-related userboxes: * {{tl|User Wiktionary}}: "This user has a page on Wiktionary" box. * {{tl|User admin Wiktionary}}: "This user is an administrator on Wiktionary" box. See also: * [[Wikipedia:Wikipedia is not a dictionary]] [[Category:Wiktionary link templates| ]] [[Category:Documentation see also templates]]

revision.comment: Bot: add {{wikidata link|Q...}} or [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]
revision.text: {| class="wikitable" style="width: auto; text-align: left;" |+ {{Navbar-header|Wiktionary link templates|Wiktionary templates common doc|plain=1}} ! Template !! Alias !! Explanation |- | {{tl|Wiktionary}} || {{tl|wikt}} || Makes a box. |- | {{tl|Wiktionary pipe}} || {{tl|wtp}} || Ditto. Allows a piped link. |- | {{tl|See Wiktionary}} || || A disambiguation hatnote type. Useful if the article title is a generic name, but the content differs from it. For example, [[:en:Tryout|Tryout]] is an article about a journal, and this template is used to link to "tryout" page in Wiktionary. |- | {{tl|Wiktionary-inline}} || {{tl|wti}} || Can be used in the "External links" section, by making a one-line navigator. Not inline in the usual sense. |- | {{tl|Wiktionary redirect}} || {{tl|wtr}} || Makes a [[Wikipedia:soft redirect|soft redirect]]. |- | {{nowrap|{{tl|Wiktionary category 2}}}} || {{tl|wtc}} || Similar to {{tl|wikt}}, but links to a category in Wiktionary. |- | {{tl|Wiktionary category}} || || Ditto, more verbose type. |- | {{tl|Linktext}} || || Turns each of consecutive words into an Wiktionary link, or any other interwiki / interlang link. Example: {{tnull|linktext|táłtłʼááh| adijiłii}}  →  {{linktext|táłtłʼááh| adijiłii}} |- | {{tl|wt}} || || Links to a language-specific section of a Wiktionary entry. Deprecated ([[Wikipedia:Templates for discussion/Log/2020 October 25#Template:Wt|discussion log]]). |- | {{tl|wikt-lang}} || || Applies a language tag to a word, and links to that language's section of the Wiktionary entry on the word, much like the [[wikt:Template:m|{{((}}m{{))}}]] and [[wikt:Template:l|{{((}}l{{))}}]] templates on Wiktionary. For instance, {{tnull|1=wikt-lang|2=en|3=be|4=was}} yields {{wikt-lang|en|be|was}}, and is equivalent to {{lang|en|[[wikt:be#English|was]]}}. |} Moving a non-encyclopedic entry to Wiktionary: * {{tl|Transwikied to Wiktionary}}, {{tl|Transwiki to Wiktionary Finished}}: Pasted at the top of the talk page by admins. Markup: * [[wikt:ᐁᕕᖃ]] → [[wikt:ᐁᕕᖃ]] * [[wikt:粘菌|'myxomycete' in Japanese]] → [[wikt:粘菌|'myxomycete' in Japanese]] (You can use a pipe as usual.) * [[wikt:curcubeu|]] → [[wikt:curcubeu|curcubeu]] (You can hide "wikt:" by a [[Help:pipe trick|pipe trick]].) Wiktionary-related userboxes: * {{tl|User Wiktionary}}: "This user has a page on Wiktionary" box. * {{tl|User admin Wiktionary}}: "This user is an administrator on Wiktionary" box. See also: * [[Wikipedia:Wikipedia is not a dictionary]] [[Category:Wiktionary link templates| ]] [[Category:Documentation see also templates]] [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]

{| class="wikitable" style="width: auto; text-align: left;" |+ {{Navbar-header|Wiktionary link templates|Wiktionary templates common doc|plain=1}} ! Template !! Alias !! Explanation |- | {{tl|Wiktionary}} || {{tl|wikt}} || Makes a box. |- | {{tl|Wiktionary pipe}} || {{tl|wtp}} || Ditto. Allows a piped link. |- | {{tl|See Wiktionary}} || || A disambiguation hatnote type. Useful if the article title is a generic name, but the content differs from it. For example, [[:en:Tryout|Tryout]] is an article about a journal, and this template is used to link to "tryout" page in Wiktionary. |- | {{tl|Wiktionary-inline}} || {{tl|wti}} || Can be used in the "External links" section, by making a one-line navigator. Not inline in the usual sense. |- | {{tl|Wiktionary redirect}} || {{tl|wtr}} || Makes a [[Wikipedia:soft redirect|soft redirect]]. |- | {{nowrap|{{tl|Wiktionary category 2}}}} || {{tl|wtc}} || Similar to {{tl|wikt}}, but links to a category in Wiktionary. |- | {{tl|Wiktionary category}} || || Ditto, more verbose type. |- | {{tl|Linktext}} || || Turns each of consecutive words into an Wiktionary link, or any other interwiki / interlang link. Example: {{tnull|linktext|táłtłʼááh| adijiłii}}  →  {{linktext|táłtłʼááh| adijiłii}} |- | {{tl|wt}} || || Links to a language-specific section of a Wiktionary entry. Deprecated ([[Wikipedia:Templates for discussion/Log/2020 October 25#Template:Wt|discussion log]]). |- | {{tl|wikt-lang}} || || Applies a language tag to a word, and links to that language's section of the Wiktionary entry on the word, much like the [[wikt:Template:m|{{((}}m{{))}}]] and [[wikt:Template:l|{{((}}l{{))}}]] templates on Wiktionary. For instance, {{tnull|1=wikt-lang|2=en|3=be|4=was}} yields {{wikt-lang|en|be|was}}, and is equivalent to {{lang|en|[[wikt:be#English|was]]}}. |} Moving a non-encyclopedic entry to Wiktionary: * {{tl|Transwikied to Wiktionary}}, {{tl|Transwiki to Wiktionary Finished}}: Pasted at the top of the talk page by admins. Wiktionary-related userboxes: * {{tl|User Wiktionary}}: "This user has a page on Wiktionary" box. * {{tl|User admin Wiktionary}}: "This user is an administrator on Wiktionary" box. revision.comment: imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Text replacement - "↵↵↵" to " "
revision.text: {| class="wikitable" style="width: auto; text-align: left;" |+ {{Navbar-header|Wiktionary link templates|Wiktionary templates common doc|plain=1}} ! Template !! Alias !! Explanation |- | {{tl|Wiktionary}} || {{tl|wikt}} || Makes a box. |- | {{tl|Wiktionary pipe}} || {{tl|wtp}} || Ditto. Allows a piped link. |- | {{tl|See Wiktionary}} || || A disambiguation hatnote type. Useful if the article title is a generic name, but the content differs from it. For example, [[:en:Tryout|Tryout]] is an article about a journal, and this template is used to link to "tryout" page in Wiktionary. |- | {{tl|Wiktionary-inline}} || {{tl|wti}} || Can be used in the "External links" section, by making a one-line navigator. Not inline in the usual sense. |- | {{tl|Wiktionary redirect}} || {{tl|wtr}} || Makes a [[Wikipedia:soft redirect|soft redirect]]. |- | {{nowrap|{{tl|Wiktionary category 2}}}} || {{tl|wtc}} || Similar to {{tl|wikt}}, but links to a category in Wiktionary. |- | {{tl|Wiktionary category}} || || Ditto, more verbose type. |- | {{tl|Linktext}} || || Turns each of consecutive words into an Wiktionary link, or any other interwiki / interlang link. Example: {{tnull|linktext|táłtłʼááh| adijiłii}}  →  {{linktext|táłtłʼááh| adijiłii}} |- | {{tl|wt}} || || Links to a language-specific section of a Wiktionary entry. Deprecated ([[Wikipedia:Templates for discussion/Log/2020 October 25#Template:Wt|discussion log]]). |- | {{tl|wikt-lang}} || || Applies a language tag to a word, and links to that language's section of the Wiktionary entry on the word, much like the [[wikt:Template:m|{{((}}m{{))}}]] and [[wikt:Template:l|{{((}}l{{))}}]] templates on Wiktionary. For instance, {{tnull|1=wikt-lang|2=en|3=be|4=was}} yields {{wikt-lang|en|be|was}}, and is equivalent to {{lang|en|[[wikt:be#English|was]]}}. |} Moving a non-encyclopedic entry to Wiktionary: * {{tl|Transwikied to Wiktionary}}, {{tl|Transwiki to Wiktionary Finished}}: Pasted at the top of the talk page by admins. Markup: * [[wikt:ᐁᕕᖃ]] → [[wikt:ᐁᕕᖃ]] * [[wikt:粘菌|'myxomycete' in Japanese]] → [[wikt:粘菌|'myxomycete' in Japanese]] (You can use a pipe as usual.) * [[wikt:curcubeu|]] → [[wikt:curcubeu|curcubeu]] (You can hide "wikt:" by a [[Help:pipe trick|pipe trick]].) Wiktionary-related userboxes: * {{tl|User Wiktionary}}: "This user has a page on Wiktionary" box. * {{tl|User admin Wiktionary}}: "This user is an administrator on Wiktionary" box. See also: * [[Wikipedia:Wikipedia is not a dictionary]] [[Category:Wiktionary link templates| ]] [[Category:Documentation see also templates]]

revision.comment: Bot: Adding dummy category to make categories work after wiki restoration
revision.text: {| class="wikitable" style="width: auto; text-align: left;" |+ {{Navbar-header|Wiktionary link templates|Wiktionary templates common doc|plain=1}} ! Template !! Alias !! Explanation |- | {{tl|Wiktionary}} || {{tl|wikt}} || Makes a box. |- | {{tl|Wiktionary pipe}} || {{tl|wtp}} || Ditto. Allows a piped link. |- | {{tl|See Wiktionary}} || || A disambiguation hatnote type. Useful if the article title is a generic name, but the content differs from it. For example, [[:en:Tryout|Tryout]] is an article about a journal, and this template is used to link to "tryout" page in Wiktionary. |- | {{tl|Wiktionary-inline}} || {{tl|wti}} || Can be used in the "External links" section, by making a one-line navigator. Not inline in the usual sense. |- | {{tl|Wiktionary redirect}} || {{tl|wtr}} || Makes a [[Wikipedia:soft redirect|soft redirect]]. |- | {{nowrap|{{tl|Wiktionary category 2}}}} || {{tl|wtc}} || Similar to {{tl|wikt}}, but links to a category in Wiktionary. |- | {{tl|Wiktionary category}} || || Ditto, more verbose type. |- | {{tl|Linktext}} || || Turns each of consecutive words into an Wiktionary link, or any other interwiki / interlang link. Example: {{tnull|linktext|táłtłʼááh| adijiłii}}  →  {{linktext|táłtłʼááh| adijiłii}} |- | {{tl|wt}} || || Links to a language-specific section of a Wiktionary entry. Deprecated ([[Wikipedia:Templates for discussion/Log/2020 October 25#Template:Wt|discussion log]]). |- | {{tl|wikt-lang}} || || Applies a language tag to a word, and links to that language's section of the Wiktionary entry on the word, much like the [[wikt:Template:m|{{((}}m{{))}}]] and [[wikt:Template:l|{{((}}l{{))}}]] templates on Wiktionary. For instance, {{tnull|1=wikt-lang|2=en|3=be|4=was}} yields {{wikt-lang|en|be|was}}, and is equivalent to {{lang|en|[[wikt:be#English|was]]}}. |} Moving a non-encyclopedic entry to Wiktionary: * {{tl|Transwikied to Wiktionary}}, {{tl|Transwiki to Wiktionary Finished}}: Pasted at the top of the talk page by admins. Markup: * [[wikt:ᐁᕕᖃ]] → [[wikt:ᐁᕕᖃ]] * [[wikt:粘菌|'myxomycete' in Japanese]] → [[wikt:粘菌|'myxomycete' in Japanese]] (You can use a pipe as usual.) * [[wikt:curcubeu|]] → [[wikt:curcubeu|curcubeu]] (You can hide "wikt:" by a [[Help:pipe trick|pipe trick]].) Wiktionary-related userboxes: * {{tl|User Wiktionary}}: "This user has a page on Wiktionary" box. * {{tl|User admin Wiktionary}}: "This user is an administrator on Wiktionary" box. See also: * [[Wikipedia:Wikipedia is not a dictionary]] [[Category:Wiktionary link templates| ]] [[Category:Documentation see also templates]] [[Category:Templates]]

revision.comment: Bot: wrap 1 stray tag(s) in noinclude [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23694499169|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text: {| class="wikitable" style="width: auto; text-align: left;" |+ {{Navbar-header|Wiktionary link templates|Wiktionary templates common doc|plain=1}} ! Template !! Alias !! Explanation |- | {{tl|Wiktionary}} || {{tl|wikt}} || Makes a box. |- | {{tl|Wiktionary pipe}} || {{tl|wtp}} || Ditto. Allows a piped link. |- | {{tl|See Wiktionary}} || || A disambiguation hatnote type. Useful if the article title is a generic name, but the content differs from it. For example, [[:en:Tryout|Tryout]] is an article about a journal, and this template is used to link to "tryout" page in Wiktionary. |- | {{tl|Wiktionary-inline}} || {{tl|wti}} || Can be used in the "External links" section, by making a one-line navigator. Not inline in the usual sense. |- | {{tl|Wiktionary redirect}} || {{tl|wtr}} || Makes a [[Wikipedia:soft redirect|soft redirect]]. |- | {{nowrap|{{tl|Wiktionary category 2}}}} || {{tl|wtc}} || Similar to {{tl|wikt}}, but links to a category in Wiktionary. |- | {{tl|Wiktionary category}} || || Ditto, more verbose type. |- | {{tl|Linktext}} || || Turns each of consecutive words into an Wiktionary link, or any other interwiki / interlang link. Example: {{tnull|linktext|táłtłʼááh| adijiłii}}  →  {{linktext|táłtłʼááh| adijiłii}} |- | {{tl|wt}} || || Links to a language-specific section of a Wiktionary entry. Deprecated ([[Wikipedia:Templates for discussion/Log/2020 October 25#Template:Wt|discussion log]]). |- | {{tl|wikt-lang}} || || Applies a language tag to a word, and links to that language's section of the Wiktionary entry on the word, much like the [[wikt:Template:m|{{((}}m{{))}}]] and [[wikt:Template:l|{{((}}l{{))}}]] templates on Wiktionary. For instance, {{tnull|1=wikt-lang|2=en|3=be|4=was}} yields {{wikt-lang|en|be|was}}, and is equivalent to {{lang|en|[[wikt:be#English|was]]}}. |} Moving a non-encyclopedic entry to Wiktionary: * {{tl|Transwikied to Wiktionary}}, {{tl|Transwiki to Wiktionary Finished}}: Pasted at the top of the talk page by admins. Markup: * [[wikt:ᐁᕕᖃ]] → [[wikt:ᐁᕕᖃ]] * [[wikt:粘菌|'myxomycete' in Japanese]] → [[wikt:粘菌|'myxomycete' in Japanese]] (You can use a pipe as usual.) * [[wikt:curcubeu|]] → [[wikt:curcubeu|curcubeu]] (You can hide "wikt:" by a [[Help:pipe trick|pipe trick]].) Wiktionary-related userboxes: * {{tl|User Wiktionary}}: "This user has a page on Wiktionary" box. * {{tl|User admin Wiktionary}}: "This user is an administrator on Wiktionary" box. See also: * [[Wikipedia:Wikipedia is not a dictionary]] [[Category:Wiktionary link templates| ]] [[Category:Documentation see also templates]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]

Example: {{tnull|linktext|táłtłʼááh| adijiłii}}  →  {{linktext|táłtłʼááh| adijiłii}} |- | {{tl|wt}} || || Links to a language-specific section of a Wiktionary entry. For instance, {{tnull|1=wikt-lang|2=en|3=be|4=was}} yields {{wikt-lang|en|be|was}}, and is equivalent to {{lang|en|[[wikt:be#English|was]]}}. |} Moving a non-encyclopedic entry to Wiktionary: * {{tl|Transwikied to Wiktionary}}, {{tl|Transwiki to Wiktionary Finished}}: Pasted at the top of the talk page by admins.
